type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
spec: Add an optional with_counts: bool = False parameter to both sync_do_groupby(value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False) and async do_groupby (same signature, keep case_sensitive if you add it). Behavior: when with_counts=False (default), preserve exact current behavior returning _GroupTuple(grouper, list) items (2-tuple-like namedtuple with fields 'grouper' and 'list'). When with_counts=True, each yielded item must instead be a 3-tuple (grouper, items, count) where items is still the list of items in that group and count is len(items) (an int). Implementation approach: after computing values = list(values) for a group (materializing the group so len() works), if with_counts: yield (key, values, len(values)) else yield _GroupTuple(key, values). If you extend _GroupTuple to a 3-field namedtuple instead, that's fine too, as long as the with_counts=False case still unpacks as exactly 2 values (grouper, items) for backward compat with existing template code doing 'for city, items in users|groupby(...)'. Also update the groupby filter's docstring to document the new with_counts parameter with a short example (e.g. '{% for grouper, list, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %}'). No changes needed in async_do_groupby beyond mirroring the same with_counts logic (it already calls auto_to_list and mirrors sync_do_groupby structure).
note: with_counts must not break existing 2-tuple unpacking when False (default)

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
spec: Add a case_sensitive: bool = False parameter to BOTH sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None) and async do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None) in src/jinja2/filters.py (~line 1160-1230). Behavior: groupby() currently sorts with the raw attrgetter key (case-sensitive), which is inconsistent with sort()'s default case-insensitive behavior. When case_sensitive=False (the default), grouping/sorting must be case-insensitive for string keys, but the OUTPUT grouper value must preserve the original case of the first item encountered in each group (not the lowercased value). Implementation approach: keep 'expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default)' as the raw attrgetter (used to extract the display/grouper value). Build a second key function for sorting+grouping: 'key = expr if case_sensitive else make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default, postprocess=ignore_case)' (ignore_case is already imported/defined in this module, used by do_sort/do_unique/do_min etc). Then do: sort the input with sorted(value, key=key); iterate itertools.groupby(sorted_value, key=key); for each (_, values) pair, materialize values into a list, and yield _GroupTuple(expr(values[0]), values) -- i.e. the grouper is expr applied to the FIRST item of that group (preserving its original case), while grouping/ordering used the case-insensitive key. For the async do_groupby, do the same but await auto_to_list(value) first and materialize 'values' via list()/await auto_to_list as currently done (values are already a plain list post itertools.groupby, no need to await again since itertools.groupby operates on the already-awaited list). Add a docstring note: ':param case_sensitive: Group upper and lower case strings separately.' and a '.. versionchanged:: (next version)  Added the case_sensitive parameter.' note. Example use case: users with city values ["CA", "NY", "ca"] should produce 2 groups by default (CA+ca merged under whichever of CA/ca sorts first alphabetically-insensitively, i.e. appears first in the case-insensitive sort order), and 3 groups when case_sensitive=True is passed.

type: CLAIM

Will send DONE when finished.
